attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
commit d5a6e7199f140d851da37d8f30a02b08fe112f32
Author: Nick Hung <nick@andestech.com>
Date:   Wed May 6 20:14:58 2026 +0800

    behavioral: add 3 C-level Tier-2 e2e fixtures linked against demo runtime
    
    Stand up a C-level analogue of the Tier-1 rt_*.S fixtures, so e2e
    tests can exercise newlib stdio, HTIF file I/O, and the demo runtime
    end-to-end through QEMU 1 -> vsim -> QEMU 2.
    
    Fixtures (all in hybrid/test/, all expect_rc 0):
    
    - rt_c_hello:    printf "hello from vsim" between drain markers,
                     confirms libgloss._write -> sim_control HTIF_PUTCHAR
                     byte-poke path.
    - rt_c_printf:   format-specifier coverage (%d %lx %s %c %p %5d
                     %08x %-10s %o %u %ld) through newlib vfprintf.
    - rt_c_file_io:  fopen/fwrite/fseek/fread/fclose/unlink round-trip
                     with in-program memcmp validation.  rt_fail() on
                     mismatch writes SimCommand::FAILED to sim_control,
                     vsim exits 1, harness reports the failure.
    
    Runtime (hybrid/test/runtime/, copied from vsim-demo verbatim except
    libgloss.c):
    
    - crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c, htif.h, syscall.h, core_v5.h, platform.h,
      memory_map.h: brings up cache/FP/V/UNA bits, vectored mtvec, .bss
      clear, and the HTIF syscall dispatch table.
    - libgloss.c: only _exit is rewritten to the RISC-V semihosting
      SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED ebreak triple.  The original demo's
      EXIT_COMMAND_ADDR write traps store-access-fault on QEMU virt's
      unassigned-MMIO and re-traps forever via mdcause, which is unmapped
      on default rv64 -- the ebreak triple is the only termination path
      QEMU 2 actually has.  vsim itself never reaches _exit (it terminates
      at the csrwi 0x7C0,1 drain marker), so dropping the MMIO write is
      safe; fail-fast inside vsim uses rt_fail() instead.
    - rt_c.ld: replaces handoff_roundtrip.ld's /DISCARD/ catch-all with
      proper .rodata/.data/.bss sections and exposes _edata/_end (consumed
      by crt0's .bss memset) and _stack=0x80700000 (sits in QEMU virt's
      default 128 MiB RAM and inside vsim's RAM).
    - rt_c_helpers.h: shared phase markers and rt_fail().
    
    Why Tier-2 (shared mmap):
    
    C frames spill ra to stack across printf/fopen calls; main()'s
    return path after csrwi 0x7C0,1 reads ra back from sp+8.  Tier-1
    gives QEMU 2 a fresh `-kernel` reload with virgin stack RAM, so ra
    loads as zero and `c.jr ra` faults to mtvec.  Tier-2 mmaps the same
    backing file across QEMU 1 / vsim / QEMU 2, so the stack survives
    the boundary intact.
    
    Wiring:
    
    - hybrid/test/Makefile: gains $(RT_C_FIXTURES) list and a per-fixture
      rule that links the runtime, includes runtime/, and passes
      -nostartfiles + -T runtime/rt_c.ld.  crt0.S is listed first so its
      .text lands at the load address and _start ends up at 0x80000000
      regardless of input order.
    - tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh: parameterized by FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC
      (defaults preserve the original rt_shared_mem behavior); QEMU 1
      spawn now passes `-cpu andes-ax45mp` so the cache/misc CSRs the
      demo crt0 writes don't trap illegal-instr.
    - tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh: same `-cpu andes-ax45mp` change for
      Tier-1 driver so it stays consistent.
    - cmake/HybridConfig.cmake: VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES foreach registers
      test_e2e_rt_shared_mem alongside the three new C fixtures.
    
    Verified:
    
    - ctest -R 'test_e2e_rt_c_': 3/3 pass.
    - ctest -R 'test_e2e_rt_(pc_jump|all_gprs|sign_bits|abi_aliases|long_roi|mem_loadstore|unaligned_lh|csr_survival|shared_mem)$':
      9/9 pass (no regression from the harness `-cpu` change or the
      parameterization of rt_shared_mem.sh).
    - Failure path verified by inverting the rt_c_file_io memcmp and
      rerunning -- harness reports `expected exit 0, got 1` and the FAIL
      message reaches vsim's stdout.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake b/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake
index eb363a7..f78f9de 100644
--- a/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake
+++ b/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake
@@ -130,12 +130,30 @@ if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
   # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
   # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
   # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
-  add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_shared_mem
-    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh)
-  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_shared_mem PROPERTIES
-    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
-    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
+  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
+    "rt_shared_mem:0"
+    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
+    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
+    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
+    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
+    "rt_c_hello:0"
+    "rt_c_printf:0"
+    "rt_c_file_io:0"
   )
+  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
+    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
+    list(GET parts 0 fname)
+    list(GET parts 1 erc)
+    add_test(
+      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
+      COMMAND bash -c

commit d5a6e7199f140d851da37d8f30a02b08fe112f32
Author: Nick Hung <nick@andestech.com>
Date:   Wed May 6 20:14:58 2026 +0800

    behavioral: add 3 C-level Tier-2 e2e fixtures linked against demo runtime
    
    Stand up a C-level analogue of the Tier-1 rt_*.S fixtures, so e2e
    tests can exercise newlib stdio, HTIF file I/O, and the demo runtime
    end-to-end through QEMU 1 -> vsim -> QEMU 2.
    
    Fixtures (all in hybrid/test/, all expect_rc 0):
    
    - rt_c_hello:    printf "hello from vsim" between drain markers,
                     confirms libgloss._write -> sim_control HTIF_PUTCHAR
                     byte-poke path.
    - rt_c_printf:   format-specifier coverage (%d %lx %s %c %p %5d
                     %08x %-10s %o %u %ld) through newlib vfprintf.
    - rt_c_file_io:  fopen/fwrite/fseek/fread/fclose/unlink round-trip
                     with in-program memcmp validation.  rt_fail() on
                     mismatch writes SimCommand::FAILED to sim_control,
                     vsim exits 1, harness reports the failure.
    
    Runtime (hybrid/test/runtime/, copied from vsim-demo verbatim except
    libgloss.c):
    
    - crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c, htif.h, syscall.h, core_v5.h, platform.h,
      memory_map.h: brings up cache/FP/V/UNA bits, vectored mtvec, .bss
      clear, and the HTIF syscall dispatch table.
    - libgloss.c: only _exit is rewritten to the RISC-V semihosting
      SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED ebreak triple.  The original demo's
      EXIT_COMMAND_ADDR write traps store-access-fault on QEMU virt's
      unassigned-MMIO and re-traps forever via mdcause, which is unmapped
      on default rv64 -- the ebreak triple is the only termination path
      QEMU 2 actually has.  vsim itself never reaches _exit (it terminates
      at the csrwi 0x7C0,1 drain marker), so dropping the MMIO write is
      safe; fail-fast inside vsim uses rt_fail() instead.
    - rt_c.ld: replaces handoff_roundtrip.ld's /DISCARD/ catch-all with
      proper .rodata/.data/.bss sections and exposes _edata/_end (consumed
      by crt0's .bss memset) and _stack=0x80700000 (sits in QEMU virt's
      default 128 MiB RAM and inside vsim's RAM).
    - rt_c_helpers.h: shared phase markers and rt_fail().
    
    Why Tier-2 (shared mmap):
    
    C frames spill ra to stack across printf/fopen calls; main()'s
    return path after csrwi 0x7C0,1 reads ra back from sp+8.  Tier-1
    gives QEMU 2 a fresh `-kernel` reload with virgin stack RAM, so ra
    loads as zero and `c.jr ra` faults to mtvec.  Tier-2 mmaps the same
    backing file across QEMU 1 / vsim / QEMU 2, so the stack survives
    the boundary intact.

    - hybrid/test/Makefile: gains $(RT_C_FIXTURES) list and a per-fixture
      rule that links the runtime, includes runtime/, and passes
      -nostartfiles + -T runtime/rt_c.ld.  crt0.S is listed first so its
      .text lands at the load address and _start ends up at 0x80000000
      regardless of input order.
    - tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh: parameterized by FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC
      (defaults preserve the original rt_shared_mem behavior); QEMU 1
      spawn now passes `-cpu andes-ax45mp` so the cache/misc CSRs the
      demo crt0 writes don't trap illegal-instr.

      Tier-1 driver so it stays consistent.

      test_e2e_rt_shared_mem alongside the three new C fixtures.

      parameterization of rt_shared_mem.sh).

commit 4d14fe7c9b27499574cb37ae1a4a6041adb16d10
Author: Nick Hung <nick@andestech.com>
Date:   Wed May 6 14:35:23 2026 +0800

    structural: register rt_pty_handback in HybridConfig CTest list
    
    Adds the new test alongside the other rt_* negative drivers so it runs
    under ctest -L hybrid-e2e in addition to run_e2e.sh.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake b/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake
index 623b4db..cf08e0f 100644
--- a/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake
+++ b/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake
@@ -139,8 +139,8 @@ if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
 
   # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
   # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
-  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer).
-  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout)
+  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
+  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
     add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
       COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
     set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
---HEAD HybridConfig---
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_shared_mem
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_shared_mem PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_shared_mem
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_shared_mem PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
3	# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
4	# artifacts in this workspace.
5	#
6	# Env overrides:
7	#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
8	#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
9	#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
10	#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
11	
12	set -euo pipefail
13	
14	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
15	VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
16	HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
17	TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"
18	
19	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
20	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
21	if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
22	  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
23	fi
24	
25	step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
26	ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
27	warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
28	fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }
29	
30	require() {
31	  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
32	    fail "$1 not found: $2"
33	    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
34	    exit 1
35	  fi
36	}
37	require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
38	require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
39	require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"
40	
41	export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN
42	
43	REQUIRED_ELFS=(
44	  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
45	  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
46	)
47	need_build=0
48	for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
49	  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
50	done
51	if (( need_build )); then
52	  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
53	  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
54	fi
55	
56	# name|command. Roundtrip fixtures share roundtrip_e2e.sh; negative drivers
57	# each have their own harness. Mirrors hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake.
58	TESTS=(
59	  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
60	  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
61	  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
62	  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
63	  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
64	  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
65	  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
66	  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
67	  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
68	  "rt_shared_mem|bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
69	  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
70	  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
71	  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
72	  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
73	)
74	
75	FILTER="${FILTER:-}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# name|command. Roundtrip fixtures share roundtrip_e2e.sh; negative drivers
# each have their own harness. Mirrors hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake.
TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem|bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"
pass=0; failed=0; skipped=0
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
for entry in "${TESTS[@]}"; do
  name="${entry%%|*}"
  cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  step "[$name]"
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd"
  rc=$?
  set -e
  case $rc in
    0)  ok   "$name PASS"; pass=$((pass+1)) ;;
    77) warn "$name (rc=77)"; skipped=$((skipped+1)) ;;
    *)  fail "$name (rc=$rc)"; failed=$((failed+1)); FAILED_NAMES+=("$name") ;;
  esac
done

echo
step "summary: $pass passed, $failed failed, $skipped skipped"
if (( failed > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
RUNTIME_DIR   := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS  := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                 $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                 -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

/* Tier-2 C-level fixture: 16x16x16 GEMM split across all three phases.
 *
 *   QEMU 1: init_matrices -- fills A and B with deterministic patterns.
 *   vsim:   matmul_kernel -- the only phase that touches sim_control via
 *           printf(); also computes C = A * B.
 *   QEMU 2: prints C row-by-row via RISC-V semihosting (SYS_WRITE0,
 *           QEMU virt has no sim_control), then re-derives the expected
 *           value of every C[i][j] and compares. Mismatch -> _exit(1).
 *
 * Cross-phase memory survival
 *   vsim's elf loader (utils/elf_loader.hpp:94) zero-fills (memsz - filesz)
 *   for every PT_LOAD on resume, which would blow away anything in .bss
 *   that QEMU 1 had written. The matrices therefore live at fixed addresses
 *   (0x80100000+) -- inside the 128 MiB shared mmap, above the ELF's last
 *   loaded byte (~0x8000d800), below the stack (0x80700000), so QEMU's
 *   -kernel load and vsim's loader both leave them untouched. The shared
 *   memory-backend-file then preserves the bytes across QEMU1 -> vsim ->
 *   QEMU2.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdio.h>

#define N 16

/* Fixed-address scratch region. Pointers (not arrays) so the compiler
 * doesn't reserve .bss for them. */
static int32_t* const A = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const B = (int32_t*) 0x80101000UL;

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, rt_c_file_io)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mp gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl, ...)
# touched by the vsim-demo crt0/handler runtime. Default rv64 illegal-traps
# on the very first mcache_ctl write. The asm fixtures (rt_shared_mem.S etc)
# don't use those CSRs but still run cleanly under the Andes CPU.
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mp \
  -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
RUNTIME_DIR   := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS  := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                 $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                 -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, rt_c_file_io)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mp gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl, ...)
# touched by the vsim-demo crt0/handler runtime. Default rv64 illegal-traps
# on the very first mcache_ctl write. The asm fixtures (rt_shared_mem.S etc)
# don't use those CSRs but still run cleanly under the Andes CPU.
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mp \
  -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)

      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_file_io:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# name|command. Roundtrip fixtures share roundtrip_e2e.sh; negative drivers
# each have their own harness. Mirrors hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake.
TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem|bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"
pass=0; failed=0; skipped=0
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
for entry in "${TESTS[@]}"; do
  name="${entry%%|*}"
  cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  step "[$name]"
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd"
  rc=$?
  set -e
  case $rc in
    0)  ok   "$name PASS"; pass=$((pass+1)) ;;
    77) warn "$name (rc=77)"; skipped=$((skipped+1)) ;;
    *)  fail "$name (rc=$rc)"; failed=$((failed+1)); FAILED_NAMES+=("$name") ;;
  esac
done

echo
step "summary: $pass passed, $failed failed, $skipped skipped"
if (( failed > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build hybrid_qemu out-of-tree under build/qemu/.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
SRC="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
BUILD="$ROOT/build/qemu"

if [[ ! -x "$SRC/configure" ]]; then
  echo "missing $SRC/configure - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

mkdir -p "$BUILD"

if [[ ! -f "$BUILD/build.ninja" && ! -f "$BUILD/Makefile" ]]; then
  (cd "$BUILD" && "$SRC/configure" \
    --target-list=riscv64-softmmu \
    --enable-plugins \
    --disable-werror \
    --disable-libnfs \
    --disable-werror)
fi

make -C "$BUILD" CFLAGS="-Wno-int-conversion -Wno-error=redundant-decls" -j64

content.content:
1	/* Drain markers and fail-fast helper shared by all C-level round-trip
2	 * fixtures (rt_c_hello.c, rt_c_printf.c, rt_c_file_io.c).
3	 *
4	 * The fixtures are timing-aware: only the span between rt_phase_qemu1_drain()
5	 * and rt_phase_vsim_drain() may touch libgloss / sim_control MMIO. Outside
6	 * that window the program runs on QEMU virt, which has no peripheral at
7	 * 0xE0080000.
8	 */
9	
10	#ifndef RT_C_HELPERS_H
11	#define RT_C_HELPERS_H
12	
13	#include <stdint.h>
14	#include <stdio.h>
15	
16	/* Matches src/platform/sim_control.hpp:32 SimCommand::FAILED. */
17	#define SIM_CONTROL_REG (*(volatile uint32_t*) 0xE0080000)
18	#define SIM_FAILED 0x01234569u
19	
20	/* Plugin- / handoff-controller-recognized drain markers (CSR 0x7C0,
21	 * uimm 0 = ENTER, uimm 1 = EXIT). Match insn_match.h. */
22	static inline void rt_phase_qemu1_drain(void)
23	{
24	  __asm__ __volatile__("csrwi 0x7C0, 0");
25	}
26	static inline void rt_phase_vsim_drain(void)
27	{
28	  __asm__ __volatile__("csrwi 0x7C0, 1");
29	}
30	
31	/* Vsim-only failure path: writes SimCommand::FAILED so vsim's
32	 * handle_sim_command (sim_control.hpp:188) sets exits=1 and the simulator
33	 * loop returns 1 before the handoff fires. The harness sees RC=1 vs
34	 * EXPECT_RC=0 and reports a failure. Bypasses QEMU2 entirely. */
35	__attribute__((noreturn)) static inline void rt_fail(void)
36	{
37	  fflush(stdout);
38	  SIM_CONTROL_REG = SIM_FAILED;
39	  for (;;) {
40	  }
41	}
42	
43	/* RISC-V semihosting calls. Only safe in the QEMU2 phase (after
44	 * rt_phase_vsim_drain): QEMU is spawned with -semihosting-config
45	 * enable=on,target=native (qemu_handback.hpp:131-132) so the magic
46	 * slli/ebreak/srai triple is intercepted before reaching mtvec. vsim
47	 * does NOT implement semihosting, so executing these inside the vsim
48	 * span would route to m_trap_entry -> exception_handler and loop. */
49	static inline void rt_semihost_writes(const char* s)
50	{
51	  register long a0 __asm__("a0") = 0x04; /* SYS_WRITE0 */
52	  register long a1 __asm__("a1") = (long) (uintptr_t) s;
53	  __asm__ __volatile__(".option push\n"
54	                       ".option norvc\n"
55	                       "slli x0, x0, 0x1f\n"
56	                       "ebreak\n"
57	                       "srai x0, x0, 0x7\n"
58	                       ".option pop\n"
59	                       :
60	                       : "r"(a0), "r"(a1)
61	                       : "memory");
62	}
63	
64	#endif /* RT_C_HELPERS_H */
